header_summary:
Recent discussions in the cryptocurrency community have centered on various strategic approaches to enhance Bitcoin's resistance to quantum computing threats. The debate highlights a proposal for EC public key recovery in P2MR (BIP-360) which could reduce witness size by 35 bytes, potentially increasing efficiency and quantum resistance ([Read more about the proposal](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/public-key-recovery-for-ec-leaves-in-p2mr-bip-360/2603)). However, there is concern that the high costs of adopting new quantum-safe outputs like P2MR and P2TRv2 could slow their uptake, as seen with previous technologies like P2TR, despite their benefits in mitigating quantum risks ([UTXO Set Report](https://research.mempool.space/utxo-set-report/) and [Bitcoin Onchain Fees UTXO Analysis](https://www.galaxy.com/insights/research/bitcoin-onchain-fees-utxo)).

In addition to quantum computing discussions, the community is exploring the potential of using inherent capabilities of quantum adversaries to authenticate their prowess without Zero-Knowledge protocols. This method simplifies interactions and reduces the risk of collision attacks in cryptographic systems, although practical implementation and the threat of adversaries waiting to compromise keys directly remain concerns ([Source details](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/zG-JBFlHqnB2ZXTgBqIPn75VUiXkjrvBrO1CMN78gccfE4BqP3LmWnxB0cu2FQItr_cLoDcMFzlWaXkv3xeUDTOZrJgEGDRvWbpvADCBAZo=@proton.me/T/#m116b880136453376b29d79f2cc42b5951d9c6c85)).

Lastly, the innovative concept of encoding art into Bitcoin transactions showcases a unique intersection of monetary policy and creativity. This method involves embedding images directly into satoshi values of transaction outputs, offering a new way to preserve and transmit art through blockchain technology. This approach not only maintains compatibility with existing Bitcoin standards but also challenges conventional data filtering techniques, pushing the boundaries of how blockchain technology can be utilized ([Explore the creative intersection](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/encoding-art-into-the-satoshi-value-field-and-the-unfilterable-floor/2604)).

recent_posts.title: Aligning privacy incentives in P2MR
recent_posts.summary: - The BIP-360 proposal will reduce witness size by eliminating 35 bytes from scripts.  - Slow adoption of quantum-safe Bitcoin outputs like P2MR and P2TRv2 is noted.  - Discussions suggest potential need for a quantum event rescue protocol in Bitcoin.

recent_posts.title: Weak Quantum Bounty Ceremony
recent_posts.summary:
- The method allows quantum adversaries to authenticate without ZK protocols.
- The model involves computing discrete logarithms to verify quantum capabilities.
- Proposed modifications include fixing \( x \) to prevent collision attacks.

recent_posts.title: Testnet 4 difficulty/hashrate
recent_posts.summary:
- The testnet4 mining scenario shows significant discrepancies in hashing difficulties.
- Nearly 90% of testnet4 block confirmations involve minimal difficulty, affecting network balance.
- The optimal ratio of real to min-difficulty blocks is around 1:10, aligning with protocol limits.

recent_posts.title: A Bitcoin-native LLM: dataset, architecture and open questions
recent_posts.summary:
- Various sources enrich Bitcoin technical discussions with expert opinions.
- Forums highlight trade-offs and emphasize respecting contributors' perspectives.
- Engaging with these resources enhances understanding of Bitcoin's technical evolution.

recent_posts.title: Public key recovery for EC leaves in P2MR (BIP-360)
recent_posts.summary:
- A vulnerability allows signature forgery in EC key tree structures.
- P2TR in Bitcoin scripting may involve complex configurations with two literal leaves.
- The spending method choice, key or script path, affects security and functionality.

recent_posts.title: Encoding art into the satoshi value field, and the unfilterable floor
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin transactions generate art by encoding image data into satoshi values.
- The encoding involves a formula and works within standard transaction rules without additional data bytes.
- Artistic pieces challenge data filtering on blockchains, intertwining monetary and artistic values.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] P2P UTXO Set Sharing
active_posts.summary: - The BIP introduces new tools for decentralized UTXO set sharing in Bitcoin.  - It aims to improve node setup times and network resilience through peer exchanges.  - The proposal outlines a verification mechanism via Merkle root comparison.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] Testnet 5
active_posts.summary: - A new draft for Testnet5 has been proposed to improve usability and test BIP54.  - Interested parties can review and provide feedback on the draft via GitHub.  - This effort is part of ongoing enhancements in the Bitcoin development community.

active_posts.title: Prohibit Merkle Internal Node Preimages That Encode Minimal 64-Byte Transactions
active_posts.summary:
- A new Bitcoin rule will invalidate blocks with specific 64-byte transaction preimages.
- This measure aims to address security issues in SPV malleability and potential ambiguities.
- The rule targets only those configurations that closely mimic Merkle node preimages.

active_posts.title: BIP-110 update: v0.4.1 release and implementation submitted to Bitcoin Core
active_posts.summary: - BIP-110 version 0.4.1, released to enhance Bitcoin by limiting transaction data sizes.  - Activation client `bitcoin-29.3.knots20260210+bip110-v0.4.1` runs stable with updated consensus rules.  - Implementation now available on multiple platforms and has significant node adoption.

active_posts.title: Bird of Prey 2: non-malleable Schnorr + PQ signatures
active_posts.summary:
- The `BoP-2` hybrid signature scheme merges Schnorr-like and arbitrary PQC schemes for enhanced security.
- `BoP-2` uses a nonce and a combined hash of messages and signatures to ensure signature integrity.
- This scheme enhances security against quantum attacks by relying on theoretical commitment properties.

active_posts.title: Default signet reorg history — was the fork at height 287766 (depth 19) deliberate?
active_posts.summary:
- The investigation into Bitcoin's testnet reveals crucial findings for developers.
- A 19-block reorg at height 287766 raises questions about its intentionality and management.
- The need for clearer documentation and reorg tracking on the testnet is emphasized.

today_in_history_posts.title: OP_BEFOREBLOCKVERIFY - discussing and opcode that invalidates a spend path after a certain block
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin developer BT has proposed a new opcode, OP_BEFOREBLOCKVERIFY (OP_BBV).
- OP_BBV allows transactions to become invalid after a certain block height.
- BT seeks feedback from the Bitcoin community on this proposal.

today_in_history_posts.title: Waiting SIGHASH_ANYPREVOUT and Packing Packages
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Matt Corallo's paper discusses pre-signed feerate issues leading to fund loss during mempool congestion.
- Bastien Teinturier's paper addresses "Pinning Attacks" on LN nodes and suggests mempool hardening solutions.
- Early deployment of "package-relay" is recommended in Bitcoin Core versions 0.24 or 0.25 to enhance LN transactions.

today_in_history_posts.title: Proof-of-work based signet faucet
today_in_history_posts.summary: - The `OP_CAT` activation enhances complex blockchain scripting and proof-of-work validation.  - Techniques prevent unauthorized claims and promote use of existing Bitcoin mining infrastructure.  - Collaborative innovations in the Bitcoin community focus on expanding scripting functionalities.
